# Application Performance Monitoring Traces

Manage OCI Application Performance Monitoring scheduled trace queries from Kubernetes with a reviewed controller-backed runtime.

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Package | `apmtraces` |
| Support Status | Preview |
| Latest Released Version | `Not yet released` |
| Install Namespace | `oci-service-operator-apmtraces-system` |
| Controller Image Pattern | `ghcr.io/<REPOSITORY_OWNER>/oci-service-operator-apmtraces:<version>` |
| Bundle Image Pattern | `ghcr.io/<REPOSITORY_OWNER>/oci-service-operator-apmtraces-bundle:<version>` |
| Setup Guide | [Open guide](../../../installation.md) |

## Notes

- Current package scope is intentionally limited to ScheduledQuery while the remaining APM Traces query and discovery families stay unpublished.
- The published runtime uses lifecycle-based generatedruntime semantics, scopes reuse by exact scheduledQueryName within one apmDomainId, and mirrors `apmDomainId` into status because OCI does not echo it in response bodies.
- No checked-in release manifest currently lists this package; the resource scope below reflects the current repository package metadata.
- This package is currently hidden from the Supported Resources landing page until a checked-in release manifest promotes it to the customer-visible surface.
